description: Hypothesis batches for the 3-body conservation law search. Batch 1 (C01-C08): initial candidates. Batch 2 (C09-C12): Z3 polynomial ring confirmed by formal checker, oracle pending. Oracle FAIL + checker PASS is the most interesting outcome.

candidates.hypothesis: Moment of inertia is approximately conserved in the figure-8 orbit
candidates.context: moment of inertia of equal-mass 3-body figure-8 orbit
candidates.expected_outcome: checker FAIL general, checker PASS figure-8 (periodic near-conservation)
candidates.motivation: conservation_checker shows frac_var=1.54e-03 on figure-8, vs >0.3 on random ICs. Near-conservation on periodic orbit.

candidates.hypothesis: Lagrange-Jacobi identity: I'' = 4E - 2V implies dI/dt is bounded, may be approximately conserved on figure-8
candidates.context: time derivative of moment of inertia in equal-mass 3-body figure-8
candidates.checker_expr: 2*np.sum(s['m'][None,:] * np.sqrt(np.sum(s['pos']**2, axis=2)) * np.sum(s['pos']*s['vel'], axis=2) / (np.sqrt(np.sum(s['pos']**2, axis=2)) + 1e-15), axis=1)
candidates.expected_outcome: checker: dI/dt oscillates around 0 on figure-8 (not conserved, but time-averaged conserved)
candidates.motivation: Lagrange-Jacobi: I'' = 4E - 2V = 2*Virial. On figure-8, Virial oscillates around 0 over period.

candidates.hypothesis: Product r12*r13*r23 is approximately conserved in figure-8 (shape invariant)
candidates.context: product of pairwise separations in equal-mass 3-body figure-8 orbit
candidates.expected_outcome: checker FAIL (frac_var=1.85e-02 on figure-8, above threshold)
candidates.motivation: Low variation on figure-8 in initial run. Tests if geometric product is structure-preserving.

candidates.hypothesis: Sqrt of sum of squared pairwise distances is conserved in Lagrange equilateral configuration
candidates.context: shape parameter of equal-mass equilateral 3-body configuration
candidates.expected_outcome: checker FAIL general; interesting if passes for specific equilateral ICs
candidates.motivation: In equilateral Lagrange solution, all r_ij are equal and constant. S would be sqrt(3)*r = const.

candidates.hypothesis: Hyperbolic excess velocity combines with angular momentum in a conserved scalar
candidates.context: Laplace-Runge-Lenz vector magnitude in Kepler orbit
candidates.expected_outcome: oracle test only; eccentricity is a conserved scalar in Kepler (not 3-body)
candidates.motivation: Eccentricity vector (LRL) is conserved in Kepler. Does anything analogous survive in restricted 3-body near resonances?

candidates.hypothesis: In a symmetric figure-8 orbit, the sum of distances from each body to the center of mass is conserved
candidates.context: sum of distances from bodies to center of mass in equal-mass figure-8 orbit
candidates.expected_outcome: checker FAIL; distances change as bodies orbit
candidates.motivation: Sanity check — should fail. Tests that the checker correctly rejects non-conserved geometric quantities.

candidates.hypothesis: Runge-Lenz-like vector: in CRTBP near L4/L5, the shape of the trojan orbit relative to the primary triangle is conserved
candidates.context: trojan asteroid distance ratio relative to primaries in CRTBP near L4
candidates.expected_outcome: oracle test only; r1/r2=1 is the exact statement of the equilateral configuration
candidates.motivation: In the Lagrange L4/L5 equilateral configuration, r1=r2=r12. Is this recoverable from the oracle?

candidates.hypothesis: In hierarchical triple (inner binary + outer body), the ratio of orbital periods is approximately conserved
candidates.context: period ratio in hierarchical triple near mean-motion resonance p:q
candidates.expected_outcome: oracle PASS (Kepler's third law is well-known); checker not applicable (orbital elements, not instantaneous state)
candidates.motivation: Kepler's third law. This SHOULD pass the oracle — sanity check that the oracle works on orbital mechanics.

candidates.hypothesis: RMS pairwise separation is approximately conserved in figure-8 (tighter than moment of inertia)
candidates.context: RMS pairwise separation in equal-mass 3-body figure-8 orbit
candidates.expected_outcome: checker PASS; oracle unknown — if FAIL, highest-priority Oracle FAIL + Checker PASS
candidates.motivation: Batch 2 result: r_rms is the tightest quadratic form in the Z3 symmetric family. frac_var=7.69e-04 on figure-8. Derived from same choreographic Z3 cancellation as r_sum.

candidates.hypothesis: Second elementary symmetric polynomial of pairwise distances is approximately conserved in figure-8
candidates.context: second elementary symmetric polynomial of pairwise distances in equal-mass 3-body figure-8
candidates.expected_outcome: checker PASS; oracle likely FAIL (non-standard form for 3-body mechanics)
candidates.motivation: Batch 2: e2 is the middle elementary symmetric polynomial. Passes at 2.69e-03. If oracle FAIL, this is our first genuine Oracle FAIL + Checker PASS candidate.

candidates.hypothesis: Geometric mean of pairwise separations is approximately conserved in figure-8
candidates.context: geometric mean of pairwise separations in equal-mass 3-body figure-8 orbit
candidates.expected_outcome: checker PASS; oracle unknown — (r12*r13*r23)^(1/3) is non-standard notation
candidates.motivation: Batch 2: monotone transform of e3 passes at 6.17e-03. The cube root stabilises the triple product and suppresses large-amplitude oscillations.

candidates.hypothesis: Z3 symmetry implies the entire ring of symmetric polynomials in {r12,r13,r23} is approximately conserved on figure-8
candidates.context: elementary symmetric polynomials of pairwise distances in 3-body choreographic orbit
candidates.truth: e1 = r12+r13+r23, e2 = r12*r13+r12*r23+r13*r23 are near-invariants; e3 = r12*r13*r23 degrades
distractors: only e1 = r12+r13+r23 is conserved; higher polynomials are not
e3 = r12*r13*r23 is conserved; e1 and e2 are not
all three e1, e2, e3 are exactly conserved
none of e1, e2, e3 are conserved in general 3-body
candidates.expected_outcome: oracle test only; meta-hypothesis summarising Z3 family
candidates.motivation: Consolidation candidate: does the oracle recognise the pattern that the entire Z3 symmetric polynomial ring degrades gracefully on the figure-8?
